servers.name: Math-MCP
servers.description: Enable your LLMs to perform accurate numerical calculations with a simple API. Leverage basic arithmetic and statistical functions to enhance your applications. Simplify complex mathematical tasks effortlessly and improve your model's capabilities.

tools.name: add
tools.description: Adds two numbers together

tools.name: subtract
tools.description: Subtracts the second number from the first number

tools.name: multiply
tools.description: Multiplies two numbers together

tools.name: division
tools.description: Divides the first number by the second number

tools.name: sum
tools.description: Adds any number of numbers together

tools.name: modulo
tools.description: Divides two numbers and returns the remainder

tools.name: mean
tools.description: Calculates the arithmetic mean of a list of numbers

tools.name: median
tools.description: Calculates the median of a list of numbers

tools.name: mode
tools.description: Finds the most common number in a list of numbers

tools.name: min
tools.description: Finds the minimum value from a list of numbers

tools.name: max
tools.description: Finds the maximum value from a list of numbers

tools.name: floor
tools.description: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer

tools.name: ceiling
tools.description: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer

tools.name: round
tools.description: Rounds a number to the nearest integer

tools.name: sin
tools.description: Calculates the sine of a number in radians

tools.name: arcsin
tools.description: Calculates the arcsine of a number in radians

tools.name: cos
tools.description: Calculates the cosine of a number in radians

tools.name: arccos
tools.description: Calculates the arccosine of a number in radians

tools.name: tan
tools.description: Calculates the tangent of a number in radians

tools.name: arctan
tools.description: Calculates the arctangent of a number in radians

tools.name: radiansToDegrees
tools.description: Converts a radian value to its equivalent in degrees

tools.name: degreesToRadians
tools.description: Converts a degree value to its equivalent in radians

servers.name: Google Sheets
servers.description: Read, write, and format spreadsheet data. Manage sheets, run formulas, and collaborate on structured data in real time.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_ADD_SHEET
tools.description: Adds a new sheet (worksheet) to a spreadsheet. Use this tool to create a new tab within an existing Google Sheet, optionally specifying its title, index, size, and other properties. IMPORTANT: - Always provide the spreadsheetId. Use 'Search Spreadsheets' action if you need to find it by name - Sheet names must be unique within the spreadsheet - For tab colors, use EITHER rgbColor OR themeColor, never both - Index must be less than or equal to the current number of sheets - CONCURRENCY WARNING: D

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_AGGREGATE_COLUMN_DATA
tools.description: Searches for rows where a specific column matches a value and performs mathematical operations on data from another column.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_APPEND_DIMENSION
tools.description: Tool to append new rows or columns to a sheet, increasing its size. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns to an existing sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_GET
tools.description: Retrieves data from specified cell ranges in a Google Spreadsheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_UPDATE
tools.description: Updates a specified range in a Google Sheet with given values, or appends them as new rows if `first_cell_location` is omitted. Auto-expands grid for large datasets, preventing range errors. Requirements: Target sheet must exist and spreadsheet must contain at least one worksheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_BATCH_UPDATE_VALUES_BY_DATA_FILTER
tools.description: Tool to update values in ranges matching data filters. Use when you need to update specific data in a Google Sheet based on criteria rather than fixed cell ranges.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CLEAR_BASIC_FILTER
tools.description: Tool to clear the basic filter from a sheet. Use when you need to remove an existing basic filter from a specific sheet within a Google Spreadsheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CLEAR_VALUES
tools.description: Clears cell content (preserving formatting and notes) from a specified A1 notation range in a Google Spreadsheet; the range must correspond to an existing sheet and cells.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_CHART
tools.description: Create a chart in a Google Sheets spreadsheet using the specified data range and chart type. Conditional requirements: - Provide either a simple chart via chart_type + data_range (basicChart), OR supply a full chart_spec supporting all chart types. Exactly one approach should be used. - When using chart_spec, set exactly one of the union fields (basicChart | pieChart | bubbleChart | candlestickChart | histogramChart | waterfallChart | treemapChart | orgChart | scorecardChart).

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_GOOGLE_SHEET1
tools.description: Creates a new Google Spreadsheet in Google Drive using the provided title.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_SPREADSHEET_COLUMN
tools.description: Creates a new column in a Google Spreadsheet, requiring a valid `spreadsheet_id` and an existing `sheet_id`; an out-of-bounds `insert_index` may append/prepend the column.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_CREATE_SPREADSHEET_ROW
tools.description: Inserts a new, empty row into a specified sheet of a Google Spreadsheet at a given index, optionally inheriting formatting from the row above.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_DELETE_DIMENSION
tools.description: Tool to delete specified rows or columns from a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to remove a range of rows or columns.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_DELETE_SHEET
tools.description: Tool to delete a sheet (worksheet) from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to remove a specific sheet from a Google Sheet document.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_EXECUTE_SQL
tools.description: Execute SQL queries against Google Sheets tables. Supports SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations with familiar SQL syntax. Tables are automatically detected and mapped from the spreadsheet structure.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_FIND_REPLACE
tools.description: Tool to find and replace text in a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to fix formula errors, update values, or perform bulk text replacements across cells. Common use cases: - Fix #ERROR! cells by replacing with empty string or correct formula - Update old values with new ones across multiple cells - Fix formula references or patterns - Clean up data formatting issues

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_FIND_WORKSHEET_BY_TITLE
tools.description: Finds a worksheet by its exact, case-sensitive title within a Google Spreadsheet; returns a boolean indicating if found and the complete metadata of the entire spreadsheet, regardless of whether the target worksheet is found.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_FORMAT_CELL
tools.description: Applies text and background cell formatting to a specified range in a Google Sheets worksheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SHEET_NAMES
tools.description: Lists all worksheet names from a specified Google Spreadsheet (which must exist), useful for discovering sheets before further operations.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_BY_DATA_FILTER
tools.description: Returns the spreadsheet at the given ID, filtered by the specified data filters. Use this tool when you need to retrieve specific subsets of data from a Google Sheet based on criteria like A1 notation, developer metadata, or grid ranges.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_GET_SPREADSHEET_INFO
tools.description: Retrieves comprehensive metadata for a Google Spreadsheet using its ID, excluding cell data.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_GET_TABLE_SCHEMA
tools.description: This action is used to get the schema of a table in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet BEFORE YOU QUERY THE TABLE. Analyze table structure and infer column names, types, and constraints. Uses statistical analysis of sample data to determine the most likely data type for each column. Call this action after calling the LIST_TABLES action to get the schema of a table in a spreadsheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_INSERT_DIMENSION
tools.description: Tool to insert new rows or columns into a sheet at a specified location. Use when you need to add empty rows or columns within an existing Google Sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_LIST_TABLES
tools.description: This action is used to list all tables in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to get the list of tables in a spreadsheet. Discover all tables in a Google Spreadsheet by analyzing sheet structure and detecting data patterns. Uses heuristic analysis to find header rows, data boundaries, and table structures.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_LOOKUP_SPREADSHEET_ROW
tools.description: Finds the first row in a Google Spreadsheet where a cell's entire content exactly matches the query string, searching within a specified A1 notation range or the first sheet by default.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_QUERY_TABLE
tools.description: This action is used to query a table in a Google Spreadsheet, call this action to query a table in a spreadsheet. Execute SQL-like SELECT queries against spreadsheet tables. Supports WHERE conditions, ORDER BY, LIMIT clauses. Call this action after calling the GET_TABLE_SCHEMA action to query a table in a spreadsheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SEARCH_DEVELOPER_METADATA
tools.description: Tool to search for developer metadata in a spreadsheet. Use when you need to find specific metadata entries based on filters.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SEARCH_SPREADSHEETS
tools.description: Search for Google Spreadsheets using various filters including name, content, date ranges, and more.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SET_BASIC_FILTER
tools.description: Tool to set a basic filter on a sheet in a Google Spreadsheet. Use when you need to filter or sort data within a specific range on a sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SHEET_FROM_JSON
tools.description: Creates a new Google Spreadsheet and populates its first worksheet from `sheet_json`, which must be non-empty as its first item's keys establish the headers.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_SHEETS_COPY_TO
tools.description: Tool to copy a single sheet from a spreadsheet to another spreadsheet. Use when you need to duplicate a sheet into a different spreadsheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_APPEND
tools.description: Tool to append values to a spreadsheet. Use when you need to add new data to the end of an existing table in a Google Sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_BATCH_CLEAR
tools.description: Tool to clear one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet. Use when you need to remove data from specific cells or ranges while keeping formatting and other properties intact.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_BATCH_CLEAR_BY_DATA_FILTER
tools.description: Clears one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet using data filters. The caller must specify the spreadsheet ID and one or more DataFilters. Ranges matching any of the specified data filters will be cleared. Only values are cleared -- all other properties of the cell (such as formatting, data validation, etc..) are kept.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_SPREADSHEETS_VALUES_BATCH_GET_BY_DATA_FILTER
tools.description: Tool to return one or more ranges of values from a spreadsheet that match the specified data filters. Use when you need to retrieve specific data sets based on filtering criteria rather than entire sheets or fixed ranges.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SHEET_PROPERTIES
tools.description: Tool to update properties of a sheet (worksheet) within a Google Spreadsheet, such as its title, index, visibility, tab color, or grid properties. Use this when you need to modify the metadata or appearance of a specific sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_UPDATE_SPREADSHEET_PROPERTIES
tools.description: Tool to update properties of a spreadsheet, such as its title, locale, or auto-recalculation settings. Use when you need to modify the overall configuration of a Google Sheet.

tools.name: GOOGLESHEETS_UPSERT_ROWS
tools.description: Upsert rows - update existing rows by key, append new ones. Automatically handles column mapping and partial updates. Use for: CRM syncs (match Lead ID), transaction imports (match Transaction ID), inventory updates (match SKU), calendar syncs (match Event ID). Features: - Auto-adds missing columns to sheet - Partial column updates (only update Phone + Status, preserve other columns) - Column order doesn't matter (auto-maps by header name) - Prevents duplicates by matching key column Example inp

servers.name: Gmail
servers.description: Manage Gmail end-to-end: send, draft, reply, forward, and bulk-modify or delete messages and threads. Organize your inbox with labels, archiving, and trashing, and retrieve messages, attachments, and profile details on demand. Access and search contacts to autofill recipients and keep people data in sync.

tools.name: GMAIL_ADD_LABEL_TO_EMAIL
tools.description: Adds and/or removes specified gmail labels for a message; ensure `message id` and all `label ids` are valid (use 'listlabels' for custom label ids).

tools.name: GMAIL_CREATE_EMAIL_DRAFT
tools.description: Creates a gmail email draft, supporting to/cc/bcc, subject, plain/html body (ensure `is html=true` for html), attachments, and threading.

tools.name: GMAIL_CREATE_LABEL
tools.description: Creates a new label with a unique name in the specified user's gmail account.

tools.name: GMAIL_DELETE_DRAFT
tools.description: Permanently deletes a specific gmail draft using its id; ensure the draft exists and the user has necessary permissions for the given `user id`.

tools.name: GMAIL_DELETE_MESSAGE
tools.description: Permanently deletes a specific email message by its id from a gmail mailbox; for `user id`, use 'me' for the authenticated user or an email address to which the authenticated user has delegated access.

tools.name: GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS
tools.description: Fetches a list of email messages from a gmail account, supporting filtering, pagination, and optional full content retrieval.

tools.name: GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_MESSAGE_ID
tools.description: Fetches a specific email message by its id, provided the `message id` exists and is accessible to the authenticated `user id`.

tools.name: GMAIL_FETCH_MESSAGE_BY_THREAD_ID
tools.description: Retrieves messages from a gmail thread using its `thread id`, where the thread must be accessible by the specified `user id`.

tools.name: GMAIL_GET_ATTACHMENT
tools.description: Retrieves a specific attachment by id from a message in a user's gmail mailbox, requiring valid message and attachment ids.

tools.name: GMAIL_GET_CONTACTS
tools.description: Fetches contacts (connections) for the authenticated google account, allowing selection of specific data fields and pagination.

tools.name: GMAIL_GET_PEOPLE
tools.description: Retrieves either a specific person's details (using `resource name`) or lists 'other contacts' (if `other contacts` is true), with `person fields` specifying the data to return.

tools.name: GMAIL_GET_PROFILE
tools.description: Retrieves key gmail profile information (email address, message/thread totals, history id) for a user.

tools.name: GMAIL_LIST_DRAFTS
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of email drafts from a user's gmail account. use verbose=true to get full draft details including subject, body, sender, and timestamp.

tools.name: GMAIL_LIST_LABELS
tools.description: Retrieves a list of all system and user-created labels for the specified gmail account.

tools.name: GMAIL_LIST_THREADS
tools.description: Retrieves a list of email threads from a gmail account, identified by `user id` (email address or 'me'), supporting filtering and pagination.

tools.name: GMAIL_MODIFY_THREAD_LABELS
tools.description: Adds or removes specified existing label ids from a gmail thread, affecting all its messages; ensure the thread id is valid.

tools.name: GMAIL_MOVE_TO_TRASH
tools.description: Moves an existing, non-deleted email message to the trash for the specified user.

tools.name: GMAIL_PATCH_LABEL
tools.description: Patches the specified label.

tools.name: GMAIL_REMOVE_LABEL
tools.description: Permanently deletes a specific, existing user-created gmail label by its id for a user; cannot delete system labels.

tools.name: GMAIL_REPLY_TO_THREAD
tools.description: Sends a reply within a specific gmail thread using the original thread's subject, requiring a valid `thread id` and correctly formatted email addresses. supports attachments via the `attachment` parameter with valid `s3key`, `mimetype`, and `name`.

servers.name: Exa Search
servers.description: Fast, intelligent web search and web crawling. Get fresh information about libraries, APIs, and SDKs.

tools.name: web_search_exa
tools.description:
Search the web for any topic and get clean, ready-to-use content.

      Best for: Finding current information, news, facts, people, companies, or answering questions about any topic.
      Returns: Clean text content from top search results.

      Query tips:
      describe the ideal page, not keywords. "blog post comparing React and Vue performance" not "React vs Vue".
      Use category:people / category:company to search through Linkedin profiles / companies respectively.
      If highlight

tools.name: web_fetch_exa
tools.description:
Read a webpage's full content as clean markdown. Use after web_search_exa when highlights are insufficient or to read any URL.

Best for: Extracting full content from known URLs. Batch multiple URLs in one call.
Returns: Clean text content and metadata from the page(s).

setup_steps.title: Smithery (hosted)
setup_steps.text:
1. Open https://smithery.ai/servers/exa
2. Click Connect and complete OAuth in your MCP client (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.)
3. MCP endpoint: https://exa.run.tools

servers.setup_summary: 1. Open https://smithery.ai/servers/exa

servers.name: Mesh MCP
servers.description: Access your network seamlessly with a simple and efficient server. Leverage a variety of tools to enhance your applications and workflows. Start integrating with your existing systems effortlessly.

tools.name: get_user_information
tools.description: Get information about the current user

tools.name: searchContacts
tools.description: Search for contacts and return matching people. Use for questions about specific contacts or "who" questions (e.g. "Who did I meet most?" or "who works as an engineer?"). Returns actual contact records for queries needing specific people. Note: interaction counts represent total accumulated interactions and cannot be filtered by date ranges.

tools.name: getContact
tools.description: Get full details for a contact by id, including emails, social links, phone numbers, and notes.

tools.name: createContact
tools.description: Create a new contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to create a completely new contact, not for adding contacts to groups.

tools.name: updateContact
tools.description: Update a contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to update a contact, not for adding contacts to groups.

tools.name: archive_contact
tools.description: Archive a contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to archive a contact.

tools.name: restore_contact
tools.description: Restore a archived contact record in Clay. This endpoint should only be used when you need to restore a contact.

tools.name: createNote
tools.description: Create a note for a contact. Only use this when the user explicitly asks to create, add, or save a note.

tools.name: getGroups
tools.description: Get all groups or lists for the user.

tools.name: createGroup
tools.description: Create a group or list for the user. If a group with the same name already exists, it will not create a duplicate unless explicitly requested to ignore the check.

tools.name: updateGroup
tools.description: Update a group or list. Use this to update the group title and/or modify its members. When adding or removing contacts, provide ALL contact IDs to add/remove in a single call - do not make multiple calls for individual contacts.

tools.name: getNotes
tools.description: Use ONLY when the user explicitly mentions "note" or "notes" to retrieve notes between two dates (e.g. "what notes from last week?"). Returns notes by creation date only - does NOT search note content or filter by other criteria. NEVER use this tool for finding contacts or any other purpose besides retrieving notes. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "note" or "notes" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

tools.name: getEvents
tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch meetings/events in a date range (e.g. "what meetings next week?", "show calendar for tomorrow"). DO NOT use for counting meetings, analyzing patterns, or finding frequent participants.

tools.name: getUpcomingEvents
tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch upcoming/next meetings/events when the user does not explicitly mention a range, and asks for upcoming or next meetings/events.

tools.name: getEmails
tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch emails in a date range (e.g. "what emails did I recieve last week?", "show emails from yesterday")

tools.name: getRecentEmails
tools.description: Use this tool ONLY to fetch recent email interactions when the user does not explicitly mention a range, and asks for past email interactions.

tools.name: getRecentReminders
tools.description: Use this tool to find recent reminders that have already occured. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "reminder" or "reminders" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

tools.name: getUpcomingReminders
tools.description: Use this tool to find recent reminders that have already occured. This tool is strictly prohibited from being used unless "reminder" or "reminders" are explicitly mentioned in the query.

tools.name: find_duplicates
tools.description: Find potential duplicate contacts.

tools.name: merge_contacts
tools.description: Merge all contact ids into one contact, this is a destructive action and can not be undone. Only run one contact group at a time and only with the users explicit permission.

servers.name: SparkForge
servers.description: Access a versatile collection of tools for media generation, web scraping, and cryptocurrency research. Convert between data formats, summarize long documents, and extract text from images or PDFs with ease. Streamline your workflow by automating tasks like SEO metadata extraction, code reviews, and content creation.

tools.name: generate_image
tools.description: Generate an AI image from a text prompt using Flux models. Returns an image URL. Models: flux-schnell (fast, $0.02), flux-dev (quality, $0.03), flux-pro (best, $0.05).

tools.name: take_screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of any webpage URL. Returns the screenshot as a base64-encoded PNG image.

tools.name: html_to_markdown
tools.description: Convert HTML content or fetch a URL and convert its HTML to clean Markdown.

tools.name: summarize_text
tools.description: Summarize long text using extractive summarization. Fast, algorithmic — no AI API call needed.

tools.name: generate_qr_code
tools.description: Generate a QR code as a base64-encoded PNG image from any text or URL.

tools.name: validate_json
tools.description: Validate and prettify JSON. Optionally validate against a JSON Schema.

tools.name: pdf_to_markdown
tools.description: Extract text from a PDF URL and convert to Markdown. Returns the text content with page count.

tools.name: get_url_metadata
tools.description: Extract metadata from a URL — title, description, Open Graph tags, favicon, canonical URL.

tools.name: get_crypto_price
tools.description: Get current cryptocurrency price, 24h change, and market cap from CoinGecko.

tools.name: detect_language
tools.description: Detect the language of a text string. Supports 10+ languages.

tools.name: text_diff
tools.description: Compare two texts and show a unified diff with additions and removals.

tools.name: btc_signal
tools.description: Get a BTC price direction prediction based on 10-indicator technical analysis consensus. Returns up/down/neutral with confidence score. Supports 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h timeframes.

tools.name: scrape_webpage
tools.description: Scrape a webpage and return clean readable text/markdown. Strips scripts, styles, and HTML tags.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web using Brave Search API. Returns titles, URLs, and descriptions.

tools.name: code_review
tools.description: Review code using Claude AI. Returns bugs, improvements, and best practice suggestions.

tools.name: write_tweet
tools.description: Generate tweet text from a topic or prompt using Claude AI. Returns tweet-ready text under 280 chars.

tools.name: verify_email
tools.description: Validate email format and check MX records for the domain.

tools.name: check_domain
tools.description: Check domain availability and DNS info via lookup.

tools.name: token_research
tools.description: Research a crypto token — description, links, price, market cap, volume from CoinGecko.

tools.name: wallet_analysis
tools.description: Analyze an Ethereum/Base wallet — ETH balance and recent transactions via Etherscan.

tools.name: crypto_news_summary
tools.description: Get latest crypto news headlines from CryptoPanic.

tools.name: generate_meme
tools.description: Generate a meme image using Imgflip API. Returns a meme image URL.

tools.name: resize_image
tools.description: Fetch an image from a URL, resize it, and return as base64 PNG.

tools.name: text_to_speech
tools.description: Convert text to speech using ElevenLabs. Returns audio as base64 MP3.

tools.name: generate_video
tools.description: Generate a short AI video from a text prompt using fal.ai.

tools.name: ocr
tools.description: Extract text from an image URL using Claude vision AI.

tools.name: site_crawl
tools.description: Crawl a website starting from a URL, follow internal links, return pages as text.

tools.name: smart_summarize
tools.description: AI-powered summarization in 4 modes: bullet_points, executive, technical, eli5.

tools.name: store_data
tools.description: Key-value data storage. Operations: set, get, delete, list. Data persists per namespace.

tools.name: extract_structured
tools.description: Extract structured JSON data from a URL using Claude AI and a provided JSON schema.

tools.name: convert_format
tools.description: Convert between formats: csv→json, json→csv, markdown→html, html→text.

tools.name: image_resize
tools.description: Fetch an image from a URL, resize it, and return as base64 PNG. Alias for resize_image.

tools.name: list_services
tools.description: List all available SparkForge services with pricing, parameters, and demo URLs.

servers.name: AI Research Assistant
servers.description:
The server provides immediate access to millions of academic papers through Semantic Scholar and arXiv, enabling AI-powered research with comprehensive search, citation analysis, and full-text PDF extraction from multiple sources (arXiv and Wiley open-access).

 - No API key is required.

tools.name: papers-search-basic
tools.description: Search for academic papers with a simple query.

tools.name: paper-search-advanced
tools.description: Search for academic papers with advanced filtering options

tools.name: search-paper-title
tools.description: Find a paper by closest title match

tools.name: get-paper-abstract
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific paper including its abstract

tools.name: papers-citations
tools.description: Get papers that cite a specific paper

tools.name: papers-references
tools.description: Get papers cited by a specific paper

tools.name: authors-search
tools.description: Search for authors by name or affiliation

tools.name: authors-papers
tools.description: Get papers written by a specific author

tools.name: papers-batch
tools.description: Look up multiple papers by their IDs

tools.name: search-arxiv
tools.description: Search for papers on arXiv using their API

tools.name: download-full-paper-arxiv
tools.description: Download full-text PDF of an arXiv paper and extract text content (memory only)

tools.name: analysis-citation-network
tools.description: Analyze the citation network for a specific paper

setup_steps.title: Smithery (hosted)
setup_steps.text:
1. Open https://smithery.ai/servers/hamid-vakilzadeh/mcpsemanticscholar
2. Click Connect and complete OAuth in your MCP client (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.)

servers.name: Google Tasks
servers.description: Google Tasks provides a simple to-do list and task management system integrated into Gmail and Google Calendar for quick and easy tracking

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_CLEAR_TASKS
tools.description: Permanently clears all completed tasks from a specified Google Tasks list; this action is destructive and idempotent.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_CREATE_TASK_LIST
tools.description: Creates a new task list with the specified title.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_DELETE_TASK
tools.description: Deletes a specified task from a given task list in Google Tasks.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_DELETE_TASK_LIST
tools.description: Permanently deletes an existing Google Task list, identified by `tasklist_id`, along with all its tasks; this operation is irreversible.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_GET_TASK
tools.description: Use to retrieve a specific Google Task if its `task_id` and parent `tasklist_id` are known.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_GET_TASK_LIST
tools.description: Retrieves a specific task list from the user's Google Tasks if the `tasklist_id` exists for the authenticated user.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_INSERT_TASK
tools.description: Creates a new task in a given `tasklist_id`, optionally as a subtask of an existing `task_parent` or positioned after an existing `task_previous` sibling, where both `task_parent` and `task_previous` must belong to the same `tasklist_id` if specified. IMPORTANT: Date fields (due, completed) accept various formats like '28 Sep 2025', '11:59 PM, 22 Sep 2025', or ISO format '2025-09-21T15:30:00Z' and will automatically convert them to RFC3339 format required by the API.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_LIST_TASK_LISTS
tools.description: Fetches the authenticated user's task lists from Google Tasks; results may be paginated.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_LIST_TASKS
tools.description: Retrieves tasks from a Google Tasks list; all date/time strings must be RFC3339 UTC, and `showCompleted` must be true if `completedMin` or `completedMax` are specified.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_MOVE_TASK
tools.description: Moves the specified task to another position in the destination task list.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_PATCH_TASK
tools.description: Partially updates an existing task (identified by `task_id`) within a specific Google Task list (identified by `tasklist_id`), modifying only the provided attributes from `TaskInput` (e.g., `title`, `notes`, `due` date, `status`) and requiring both the task and list to exist.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_PATCH_TASK_LIST
tools.description: Updates the title of an existing Google Tasks task list.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_UPDATE_TASK
tools.description: Updates the specified task.

tools.name: GOOGLETASKS_UPDATE_TASK_LIST
tools.description: Updates the authenticated user's specified task list.

servers.name: Brave Search
servers.description: Search the web with Brave's independent index — web, news, images, and videos. Bring your own subscription token from the [Brave Search API dashboard](https://api-dashboard.search.brave.com).

tools.name: brave_web_search
tools.description: Performs web searches using the Brave Search API and returns comprehensive search results with rich metadata. To chain into local-POI enrichment, pass `result_filter=locations` and feed the resulting `locations.results[].id` values into `brave_local_search`. To chain into the AI summarizer, pass `summary=true` and feed the returned `summarizer.key` into `brave_summarizer`.

tools.name: brave_news_search
tools.description: Searches for news articles using Brave's News Search API. Use it when you need current news, breaking updates, or articles about specific topics.

tools.name: brave_image_search
tools.description: Performs an image search using the Brave Search API. Helpful when you need pictures of people, places, things, graphic-design ideas, or art inspiration. Counts of up to 100 are supported.

tools.name: brave_video_search
tools.description: Searches for videos using Brave's Video Search API. Returns structured video results with metadata.

tools.name: brave_local_search
tools.description: Brave Local Search API returns enriched information (address, phone, hours, rating) for location-search results. Access requires the Brave Search API Pro plan; currently US-only. Two-step flow: first call `brave_web_search` with `result_filter=locations` to obtain `locations.results[].id`, then pass them here. NOTE: This tool takes location IDs from a prior web-search response; if you have a free-text query, call `brave_web_search` first.

tools.name: brave_llm_context
tools.description: Retrieves pre-extracted, relevance-ranked web content using Brave's LLM Context API, optimized for AI agents, LLM grounding, and RAG pipelines. Unlike a traditional web search that returns links and short descriptions, this tool returns the actual substance of matching pages — text chunks, tables, code blocks, and structured data — so the model can reason over it directly. When relaying results in markdown-supporting environments, cite source URLs from the `sources` map.

tools.name: brave_place_search
tools.description: Searches Brave's Place Search API. A single call may populate any combination of `results` (POIs), `cities`, `addresses`, `streets`, and `location` (the resolved search area), depending on the query shape. Use for POIs near coordinates or a named area, browsing general POIs (omit `query`, supply coords or `location`), disambiguating bare city names, resolving specific addresses, or looking up streets. Anchor via `latitude`+`longitude` or `location`; with neither, `query` is required.

tools.name: brave_summarizer
tools.description: Retrieves AI-generated summaries of web search results. Two-step flow: first call `brave_web_search` with `summary=true` to obtain `summarizer.key`, then pass it here. Pro AI tier required.

servers.name: Google Calendar
servers.description: Schedule events, check availability, and manage calendars. Create meetings, set reminders, and coordinate across time zones.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_ACL_PATCH
tools.description: Updates an access control rule for a calendar using patch semantics (partial update). This allows modifying specific fields without affecting other properties. Note: Each patch request consumes three quota units. For domain-type ACL rules, if PATCH fails with 500 error, this action will automatically fallback to UPDATE method.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_INSERT
tools.description: Inserts an existing calendar into the user's calendar list.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDAR_LIST_UPDATE
tools.description: Updates an existing entry on the user\'s calendar list.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDARS_DELETE
tools.description: Deletes a secondary calendar. Use calendars.clear for clearing all events on primary calendars.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CALENDARS_UPDATE
tools.description: Updates metadata for a calendar.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CLEAR_CALENDAR
tools.description: Clears a primary calendar. This operation deletes all events associated with the primary calendar of an account.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_CREATE_EVENT
tools.description: Create a Google Calendar event using `start_datetime` plus `event_duration_hour` and `event_duration_minutes` fields to derive the end time. Requires calendar write access. The organizer is added as an attendee unless `exclude_organizer` is True. Example request to create event for 1 hour 30 minutes: { "calendar_id": "primary", "start_datetime": "2025-01-16T13:00:00", "timezone": "America/New_York", "event_duration_hour": 1, "event_duration_minutes": 30, "summary": "Client sync", "attendees": [{

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_DELETE_EVENT
tools.description: Deletes a specified event by `event_id` from a Google Calendar (`calendar_id`); this action is idempotent and raises a 404 error if the event is not found.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_DUPLICATE_CALENDAR
tools.description: Creates a new, empty Google Calendar with the specified title (summary).

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_INSTANCES
tools.description: Returns instances of the specified recurring event.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST
tools.description: Returns events on the specified calendar.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_MOVE
tools.description: Moves an event to another calendar, i.e., changes an event's organizer.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_WATCH
tools.description: Watch for changes to Events resources.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_EVENT
tools.description: Finds events in a specified Google Calendar using text query, time ranges (event start/end, last modification), and event types; ensure `timeMin` is not chronologically after `timeMax` if both are provided.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_FIND_FREE_SLOTS
tools.description: Finds both free and busy time slots in Google Calendars for specified calendars within a defined time range (defaults to the current day UTC if `time_min`/`time_max` are omitted). Returns busy intervals enriched with event details and calculates free slots by finding gaps between busy periods; `time_min` must precede `time_max` if both are provided. This action retrieves free and busy time slots for the specified calendars over a given time period. It analyzes the busy intervals from the calenda

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_FREE_BUSY_QUERY
tools.description: Returns free/busy information for a set of calendars.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_GET_CALENDAR
tools.description: Retrieves a specific Google Calendar, identified by `calendar_id`, to which the authenticated user has access.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_GET_CURRENT_DATE_TIME
tools.description: Gets the current date and time, allowing for a specific timezone offset.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_ACL_RULES
tools.description: Retrieves the list of access control rules (ACLs) for a specified calendar, providing the necessary 'rule_id' values required for updating specific ACL rules.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_CALENDARS
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of calendars from the user's calendar list, with optional filtering and sync capabilities.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_CALENDAR
tools.description: Partially updates (PATCHes) an existing Google Calendar, modifying only the fields provided; `summary` is mandatory and cannot be an empty string, and an empty string for `description` or `location` clears them.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_PATCH_EVENT
tools.description: Update specified fields of an existing event in a Google Calendar using patch semantics (array fields like `attendees` are fully replaced if provided); ensure the `calendar_id` and `event_id` are valid and the user has write access to the calendar.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_QUICK_ADD
tools.description: Parses natural language text to quickly create a basic Google Calendar event with its title, date, and time, suitable for simple scheduling; does not support direct attendee addition or recurring events, and `calendar_id` must be valid if not 'primary'.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_REMOVE_ATTENDEE
tools.description: Removes an attendee from a specified event in a Google Calendar; the calendar and event must exist.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_SETTINGS_LIST
tools.description: Returns all user settings for the authenticated user.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_SETTINGS_WATCH
tools.description: Watch for changes to Settings resources.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_SYNC_EVENTS
tools.description: Synchronizes Google Calendar events, performing a full sync if no `sync_token` is provided or if a 410 GONE error (due to an expired token) necessitates it, otherwise performs an incremental sync for events changed since the `sync_token` was issued.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_UPDATE_ACL_RULE
tools.description: Updates an access control rule for the specified calendar.

tools.name: GOOGLECALENDAR_UPDATE_EVENT
tools.description: Updates an existing event by `event_id` in a Google Calendar; this is a full PUT replacement, so provide all desired fields as unspecified ones may be cleared or reset.

tools.name: get_player_splits
tools.description:
Returns a dataframe of all split stats for a given player.
    If player_info is True, this will also return a dictionary that includes player position,
        handedness, height, weight, position, and team

tools.name: get_pybaseball_standings
tools.description:
Returns a pandas DataFrame of the standings for a given MLB season, or the most recent standings
    if the date is not specified.

    ARGUMENTS
        season (int): the year of the season

tools.name: get_team_batting
tools.description:
Get season-level Batting Statistics for Specific Team (from Baseball-Reference)

    ARGUMENTS:
    team : str : The Team Abbreviation (i.e. 'NYY' for Yankees) of the Team you want data for
    start_season : int : first season you want data for
        (or the only season if you do not specify an end_season)
    end_season : int : final season you want data for

tools.name: get_team_fielding
tools.description:
Get season-level Fielding Statistics for Specific Team (from Baseball-Reference)

    ARGUMENTS:
    team            : str : The Team Abbreviation (i.e., 'NYY' for Yankees) of the
        Team you want data for
    start_season    : int : first season you want data for
        (or the only season if you do not specify an end_season)
    end_season      : int : final season you want data for

tools.name: get_team_pitching
tools.description:
Get season-level Pitching Statistics for Specific Team (from Baseball-Reference)

    ARGUMENTS:
    team : str : The Team Abbreviation (i.e. 'NYY' for Yankees) of the Team you want data for
    start_season : int : first season you want data for
        (or the only season if you do not specify an end_season)
    end_season : int : final season you want data for

tools.name: get_top_prospects
tools.description:
Retrieves the top prospects by team or leaguewide.
        It can return top prospect pitchers, batters, or both.

    ARGUMENTS
    team: The team name for which you wish to retrieve top prospects. If not specified,
        the function will return leaguewide top prospects.
    playerType: Either "pitchers" or "batters".
        If not specified, the function will return top prospects for both
        pitchers and batters.

servers.description: Reddit is a social news platform with user-driven communities (subreddits), offering content sharing, discussions, and viral marketing opportunities for brands

tools.name: REDDIT_CREATE_REDDIT_POST
tools.description: Creates a new text or link post on a specified, existing Reddit subreddit, optionally applying a flair.

tools.name: REDDIT_DELETE_REDDIT_COMMENT
tools.description: Deletes a Reddit comment, identified by its fullname ID, if it was authored by the authenticated user.

tools.name: REDDIT_DELETE_REDDIT_POST
tools.description: Permanently deletes a Reddit post by its ID, provided the authenticated user has deletion permissions for that post.

tools.name: REDDIT_EDIT_REDDIT_COMMENT_OR_POST
tools.description: Edits the body text of the authenticated user's own existing comment or self-post on Reddit; cannot edit link posts or titles.

tools.name: REDDIT_GET_USER_FLAIR
tools.description: Fetches the list of available link flairs (i.e., post flairs, not user flairs) for a given subreddit.

tools.name: REDDIT_POST_REDDIT_COMMENT
tools.description: Posts a comment on Reddit, replying to an existing and accessible submission (post) or another comment.

tools.name: REDDIT_RETRIEVE_POST_COMMENTS
tools.description: Retrieves all comments for a Reddit post given its article ID (which must be for an existing, public post); nested replies within comments are returned as raw dictionaries requiring parsing.

tools.name: REDDIT_RETRIEVE_REDDIT_POST
tools.description: Retrieves the current hot posts from a specified, publicly accessible subreddit.

tools.name: REDDIT_RETRIEVE_SPECIFIC_COMMENT
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for a specific Reddit comment or post using its fullname.

tools.name: REDDIT_SEARCH_ACROSS_SUBREDDITS
tools.description: Searches Reddit for content (e.g., posts, comments) using a query, with results typically confined to subreddits unless `restrict_sr` is set to False.

servers.name: UnClick
servers.description: AI agent tool marketplace. 60+ tools for social, e-commerce, accounting, messaging, media, and more. Search and call tools for Slack, Reddit, Discord, Shopify, Xero, Bluesky, TMDB, Steam, and 50+ more services.

tools.name: unclick_search
tools.description: Search the UnClick tool marketplace by keyword or description. Use this to discover which tools are available for a task. Example: 'I need to resize an image' → returns the image tool with its endpoints.

tools.name: unclick_browse
tools.description: Browse all available UnClick tools, optionally filtered by category. Returns a list of tools with their slugs and descriptions.

tools.name: unclick_tool_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific UnClick tool including all its endpoints, required parameters, and response shapes. Use this after unclick_search to understand exactly how to call a tool.

tools.name: unclick_call
tools.description: Call any UnClick tool endpoint. Specify the endpoint ID and parameters. Use unclick_search or unclick_tool_info to discover endpoint IDs and required params. Example: endpoint_id='image.resize', params={image: '<base64>', width: 800, height: 600}

tools.name: unclick_shorten_url
tools.description: Shorten a URL using UnClick. Returns a short URL and its code.

tools.name: unclick_generate_qr
tools.description: Generate a QR code from text or a URL. Returns base64-encoded PNG or SVG.

tools.name: unclick_hash
tools.description: Compute a cryptographic hash (MD5, SHA1, SHA256, SHA512) of text.

tools.name: unclick_transform_text
tools.description: Transform text case: upper, lower, title, sentence, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case, PascalCase.

tools.name: unclick_validate_email
tools.description: Validate an email address format.

tools.name: unclick_validate_url
tools.description: Validate a URL format, optionally check if it's reachable.

tools.name: unclick_resize_image
tools.description: Resize an image (provided as base64) to specified dimensions.

tools.name: unclick_parse_csv
tools.description: Parse a CSV string into a JSON array of rows.

tools.name: unclick_json_format
tools.description: Format / pretty-print a JSON string.

tools.name: unclick_encode
tools.description: Encode or decode text. Supports base64, URL, HTML, and hex.

tools.name: unclick_generate_uuid
tools.description: Generate one or more random UUIDs (v4).

tools.name: unclick_random_password
tools.description: Generate a secure random password.

tools.name: unclick_cron_parse
tools.description: Convert a cron expression to a human-readable description and get next occurrences.

tools.name: unclick_ip_parse
tools.description: Parse an IP address — get decimal, binary, hex, and type (private/loopback/multicast).

tools.name: unclick_color_convert
tools.description: Convert a color between hex, RGB, HSL, and HSV formats.

tools.name: unclick_regex_test
tools.description: Test a regex pattern against text and get all matches with groups.

tools.name: unclick_timestamp_convert
tools.description: Convert a timestamp (ISO, Unix seconds, or Unix ms) to all common formats.

tools.name: unclick_diff_text
tools.description: Compare two strings and return a unified diff showing what changed.

tools.name: unclick_kv_set
tools.description: Store a value in the UnClick key-value store with optional TTL.

tools.name: unclick_kv_get
tools.description: Retrieve a value from the UnClick key-value store.

tools.name: report_bug
tools.description: Report a bug or unexpected behavior encountered while using an UnClick tool. Call this whenever a tool returns an error, behaves unexpectedly, or fails silently. Severity is auto-classified from the error message: 500/fatal → critical, timeout/503 → high, 4xx/invalid → low, everything else → medium.

servers.name: Slack
servers.description: Slack is a channel-based messaging platform. With Slack, people can work together more effectively, connect all their software tools and services, and find the information they need to do their best work — all within a secure, enterprise-grade environment.

tools.name: SLACK_ACTIVATE_OR_MODIFY_DO_NOT_DISTURB_DURATION
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Turns on Do Not Disturb mode for the current user, or changes its duration. Use `set_dnd_duration` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_A_CUSTOM_EMOJI_TO_A_SLACK_TEAM
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Adds a custom emoji to a Slack workspace given a unique name and an image URL. Use `add_emoji` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_AN_EMOJI_ALIAS_IN_SLACK
tools.description: Adds an alias for an existing custom emoji in a Slack Enterprise Grid organization.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_A_REMOTE_FILE_FROM_A_SERVICE
tools.description: Adds a reference to an external file (e.g., Google Drive, Dropbox) to Slack for discovery and sharing, requiring a unique `external_id` and an `external_url` accessible by Slack.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_A_STAR_TO_AN_ITEM
tools.description: Stars a channel, file, file comment, or a specific message in Slack.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_CALL_PARTICIPANTS
tools.description: Registers new participants added to a Slack call.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_EMOJI
tools.description: Adds a custom emoji to a Slack workspace given a unique name and an image URL; subject to workspace emoji limits.

tools.name: SLACK_ADD_REACTION_TO_AN_ITEM
tools.description: Adds a specified emoji reaction to an existing message in a Slack channel, identified by its timestamp; does not remove or retrieve reactions.

tools.name: SLACK_ARCHIVE_A_PUBLIC_OR_PRIVATE_CHANNEL
tools.description: Archives a Slack public or private channel, making it read-only; the primary 'general' channel cannot be archived.

tools.name: SLACK_ARCHIVE_A_SLACK_CONVERSATION
tools.description: Archives a Slack conversation by its ID, rendering it read-only and hidden while retaining history, ideal for cleaning up inactive channels; be aware that some channels (like #general or certain DMs) cannot be archived and this may impact connected integrations.

tools.name: SLACK_CHAT_POST_MESSAGE
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Posts a message to a Slack channel, direct message, or private channel. Use `send_message` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_CLEAR_STATUS
tools.description: Clears the Slack status for the authenticated user, removing any custom text and emoji.

tools.name: SLACK_CLOSE_DM_OR_MULTI_PERSON_DM
tools.description: Closes a Slack direct message (DM) or multi-person direct message (MPDM) channel, removing it from the user's sidebar without deleting history; this action affects only the calling user's view.

tools.name: SLACK_CREATE_A_REMINDER
tools.description: Creates a Slack reminder with specified text and time; time accepts Unix timestamps, seconds from now, or natural language (e.g., 'in 15 minutes', 'every Thursday at 2pm').

tools.name: SLACK_CREATE_A_SLACK_USER_GROUP
tools.description: Creates a new User Group (often referred to as a subteam) in a Slack workspace.

tools.name: SLACK_CREATE_CANVAS
tools.description: Creates a new Slack Canvas with the specified title and optional content.

tools.name: SLACK_CREATE_CHANNEL
tools.description: Initiates a public or private channel-based conversation

tools.name: SLACK_CREATE_CHANNEL_BASED_CONVERSATION
tools.description: Creates a new public or private Slack channel with a unique name; the channel can be org-wide, or team-specific if `team_id` is given (required if `org_wide` is false or not provided).

tools.name: SLACK_CUSTOMIZE_URL_UNFURL
tools.description: Customizes URL previews (unfurling) in a specific Slack message using a URL-encoded JSON in `unfurls` to define custom content or remove existing previews.

tools.name: SLACK_CUSTOMIZE_URL_UNFURLING_IN_MESSAGES
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Customizes URL previews (unfurling) in a specific Slack message. Use `customize_url_unfurl` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_A_COMMENT_ON_A_FILE
tools.description: Deletes a specific comment from a file in Slack; this action is irreversible.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_A_FILE_BY_ID
tools.description: Permanently deletes an existing file from a Slack workspace using its unique file ID; this action is irreversible and also removes any associated comments or shares.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_A_PUBLIC_OR_PRIVATE_CHANNEL
tools.description: Permanently and irreversibly deletes a specified public or private channel, including all its messages and files, within a Slack Enterprise Grid organization.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_A_SCHEDULED_MESSAGE_IN_A_CHAT
tools.description: Deletes a pending, unsent scheduled message from the specified Slack channel, identified by its `scheduled_message_id`.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_A_SLACK_REMINDER
tools.description: Deletes an existing Slack reminder, typically when it is no longer relevant or a task is completed; this operation is irreversible.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_CANVAS
tools.description: Deletes a Slack Canvas permanently.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETES_A_MESSAGE_FROM_A_CHAT
tools.description: Deletes a message, identified by its channel ID and timestamp, from a Slack channel, private group, or direct message conversation; the authenticated user or bot must be the original poster.

tools.name: SLACK_DELETE_USER_PROFILE_PHOTO
tools.description: Deletes the Slack profile photo for the user identified by the token, reverting them to the default avatar; this action is irreversible and succeeds even if no custom photo was set.

tools.name: SLACK_DISABLE_AN_EXISTING_SLACK_USER_GROUP
tools.description: Disables a specified, currently enabled Slack User Group by its unique ID, effectively archiving it by setting its 'date_delete' timestamp; the group is not permanently deleted and can be re-enabled.

tools.name: SLACK_EDIT_CANVAS
tools.description: Edits a Slack Canvas with granular control over content placement. Supports replace, insert (before/after/start/end) operations for flexible content management.

tools.name: SLACK_ENABLE_A_SPECIFIED_USER_GROUP
tools.description: Enables a disabled User Group in Slack using its ID, reactivating it for mentions and permissions; this action only changes the enabled status and cannot create new groups or modify other properties.

tools.name: SLACK_ENABLE_PUBLIC_SHARING_OF_A_FILE
tools.description: Enables public sharing for an existing Slack file by generating a publicly accessible URL; this action does not create new files.

tools.name: SLACK_END_A_CALL_WITH_DURATION_AND_ID
tools.description: Ends an ongoing Slack call, identified by its ID (obtained from `calls.add`), optionally specifying the call's duration.

tools.name: SLACK_END_SNOOZE
tools.description: Ends the current user's snooze mode immediately.

tools.name: SLACK_END_USER_DO_NOT_DISTURB_SESSION
tools.description: Ends the authenticated user's current Do Not Disturb (DND) session in Slack, affecting only DND status and making them available; if DND is not active, Slack acknowledges the request without changing status.

tools.name: SLACK_END_USER_SNOOZE_MODE_IMMEDIATELY
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Ends the current user's snooze mode immediately. Use `end_snooze` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_BOT_USER_INFORMATION
tools.description: Fetches information for a specified, existing Slack bot user; will not work for regular user accounts or other integration types.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_CONVERSATION_HISTORY
tools.description: Fetches a chronological list of messages and events from a specified Slack conversation, accessible by the authenticated user/bot, with options for pagination and time range filtering.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_CURRENT_TEAM_INFO_WITH_OPTIONAL_TEAM_SCOPE
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Fetches comprehensive metadata about the current Slack team. Use `fetch_team_info` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_DND_STATUS_FOR_MULTIPLE_TEAM_MEMBERS
tools.description: DEPRECATED: Retrieves a user's current Do Not Disturb status. Use `get_team_dnd_status` instead.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_ITEM_REACTIONS
tools.description: Fetches reactions for a Slack message, file, or file comment, requiring one of: channel and timestamp; file ID; or file comment ID.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_MESSAGE_THREAD_FROM_A_CONVERSATION
tools.description: Retrieves replies to a specific parent message in a Slack conversation, using the channel ID and the parent message's timestamp (`ts`).

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_TEAM_INFO
tools.description: Fetches comprehensive metadata about the current Slack team, or a specified team if the provided ID is accessible.

tools.name: SLACK_FETCH_WORKSPACE_SETTINGS_INFORMATION
tools.description: Retrieves detailed settings for a specific Slack workspace, primarily for administrators in an Enterprise Grid organization to view or audit workspace configurations.

tools.name: SLACK_FIND_CHANNELS
tools.description: Find channels in a Slack workspace by any criteria - name, topic, purpose, or description.

tools.name: SLACK_FIND_USER_BY_EMAIL_ADDRESS
tools.description: Retrieves the Slack user object for an active user by their registered email address; fails with 'users_not_found' if the email is unregistered or the user is inactive.

tools.name: SLACK_FIND_USERS
tools.description: Find users in a Slack workspace by any criteria - email, name, display name, or other text. Includes optimized email lookup for exact email matches.

tools.name: SLACK_GET_CANVAS
tools.description: Retrieves a specific Slack Canvas by its ID, including its content and metadata.

tools.name: SLACK_GET_CHANNEL_CONVERSATION_PREFERENCES
tools.description: Retrieves conversation preferences (e.g., who can post, who can thread) for a specified channel, primarily for use within Slack Enterprise Grid environments.

tools.name: SLACK_GET_REMINDER_INFORMATION
tools.description: Retrieves detailed information for an existing Slack reminder specified by its ID; this is a read-only operation.

tools.name: bgg-recommender

tools.name: bgg-rules

tools.name: bgg-thread-details

servers.name: Context7
servers.description: Fetch up-to-date, version-specific documentation and code examples directly into your prompts. Enhance your coding experience by eliminating outdated information and hallucinated APIs. Simply add `use context7` to your questions for accurate and relevant answers.

tools.name: resolve-library-id
tools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'query-docs' to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact

tools.name: query-docs
tools.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'resolve-library-id' first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best information

servers.name: romulus-31780
servers.description:
ROMulus is a retro gaming ROM search engine that aggregates results from four major sites. Search by game title and console to get download page URLs, cover images, and file sizes... all in one all.

 Tools:
 • search_roms — Find ROMs by title, optionally filtered by console (NES, SNES, N64, GameCube, PS1,
   PS2, PSP, GBA, Genesis, Dreamcast, and 20+ more)
 • get_game_details — Fetch full metadata for a game: year, region, genre, languages, rating,
  serial number, and description
 • list_systems — List all 28 supported consoles

 No API key required.

tools.name: search_roms
tools.description:
Search for ROM download pages across Vimm's Lair, ROMsGames, CDRomance, and LoLROMs.

    Args:
        query: Game title to search for (e.g. 'Super Mario Bros', 'Zelda')
        system: Console to filter by. Use 'all' to search all systems.
                Supported: nes, snes, n64, gamecube, wii, gb, gbc, gba, ds, 3ds,
                ps1, ps2, ps3, psp, genesis, dreamcast, saturn, sms, gg, tg16, xbox, all

tools.name: get_game_details
tools.description:
Fetch full details for a specific game given its ROM page URL.

    Returns size, year, region, genre, languages, rating, serial number,
    description, and cover image.

    Args:
        url: The game page URL from a search_roms result
             (vimm.net, romsgames.net, cdromance.org, or lolroms.com)

tools.name: list_systems
tools.description: List all supported gaming systems/consoles you can search ROMs for.

servers.name: OneSignal
servers.description:
OneSignal is a customer engagement platform that lets you send targeted push notifications, emails, SMS, and in-app messages, manage audiences, and track campaign performance.

With the OneSignal MCP, manage your messaging directly from your AI assistant. Send push notifications, emails, and SMS by simply describing your audience and message. Check campaign delivery stats, build audience segments, manage templates, and handle user subscriptions all through conversation.

tools.name: create_alias_by_subscription
tools.description: Create or update identity aliases for the user who owns a given subscription. The `onesignal_id` alias is read-only and must not be included.

tools.name: create_or_update_alias
tools.description: Create or update identity aliases for a user identified by alias. The `onesignal_id` alias is read-only and must not be included.

tools.name: create_segment
tools.description: Create a new audience segment with filter conditions. Maximum 200 filter entries.

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.description: Create a new subscription and attach it to a user. Set `type` to the channel ("Email", "SMS", "iOSPush", "AndroidPush", etc.) and `token` to the email address, E.164 phone number, or push token.

tools.name: create_template
tools.description: Create a new notification template for a OneSignal app.

tools.name: create_user
tools.description: Create a new user in a OneSignal app. Provide an `identity` (e.g. {"external_id": "user-123"}) so the user can be referenced later by alias. Optionally attach `properties` (tags, language, country) and `subscriptions` (email, SMS, or push channels). The `identity` field is required to prevent orphaned users.

tools.name: export_audience_activity_csv
tools.description: Export audience activity for a notification to CSV. WARNING: Only 1 concurrent export is allowed per account — if a 409 or 429 is returned, a previous export is still running.

tools.name: export_subscriptions_csv
tools.description: Export all subscriptions for a OneSignal app to CSV. WARNING: Only 1 concurrent export is allowed per account — if a 409 or 429 is returned, a previous export is still running.

tools.name: get_segment
tools.description: Retrieve a single audience segment by ID. By default, includes full segment metadata and filters (`payload` object with `id`, `name`, `created_at`, `source`, and `filters`). Set `include_segment_detail` to `false` to return only the subscriber count. Note: the API returns 400 for user-based segments (those using custom_event or message_event filters).

tools.name: get_template
tools.description: Retrieve a single notification template by ID.

tools.name: get_user_identity
tools.description: Retrieve all identity aliases associated with a user. Identify the user by `alias_label` (typically "external_id") and `alias_id`.

tools.name: get_user_identity_by_subscription
tools.description: Retrieve the identity aliases for the user who owns a given subscription.

tools.name: list_messages
tools.description: List push notifications for a OneSignal app. Use `limit` (default 50, max ~250) and `offset` for pagination. Avoid large offsets — this endpoint has known performance degradation at high page numbers.

tools.name: list_segments
tools.description: List audience segments for a OneSignal app. Maximum 300 segments per page.

tools.name: list_templates
tools.description: List notification templates for a OneSignal app. Maximum 50 per page.

tools.name: onesignal_config
tools.description: Return the current OneSignal MCP server configuration and connected app details.

tools.name: onesignal_health
tools.description: Check the health status of the OneSignal MCP server.

tools.name: onesignal_reference_overview
tools.description: Return an overview of the OneSignal REST API reference, including available endpoints and rate limits.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a push notification, email, or SMS via the OneSignal API. TIER 3 — HIGH IMPACT: confirmation is required before sending. Rate limited to 10 requests per minute. The `filters` array is limited to 200 entries; max 20,000 users per call. For push: set `contents`. For email: set `email_subject` + `email_body` (or use `template_id`). For SMS: set `contents` + `target_channel = "sms"`.

tools.name: start_live_activity
tools.description: Start an iOS Live Activity and deliver it to a specific device subscription.

tools.name: transfer_subscription
tools.description: Transfer a subscription to a different user within the same app. Cross-app transfers are not supported. Rate limited to 1 request per second per subscription.

tools.name: unsubscribe_email
tools.description: Unsubscribe an email address using the token from an email unsubscribe link. This endpoint uses token-based auth (no REST API key required).

tools.name: update_live_activity
tools.description: Update or end an active iOS Live Activity.

tools.name: update_segment
tools.description: Update an existing audience segment's name and/or filter conditions. `name` is always required even if not changing it (max 128 characters). When `filters` is provided it replaces all existing filters; omit to keep existing filters intact. Maximum 200 filter entries.

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.description: Update an existing subscription (token, enabled status, or test type).

tools.name: update_subscription_by_token
tools.description: Update a subscription identified by its channel type and token. Provide `subscription_type` and `token` to identify the subscription, then pass the fields to update in `subscription`.

tools.name: update_template
tools.description: Update an existing notification template. The `name` field is required even if you are not changing it.

tools.name: update_user
tools.description: Update an existing user identified by alias. Use `properties` to set tags, language, or country. Use `deltas` to increment session counts or append purchase records (these add to existing values rather than overwriting). Returns 202 — the update is processed asynchronously.

tools.name: view_message
tools.description: Retrieve a single notification by ID.

tools.name: view_outcomes
tools.description: Retrieve outcome statistics for a OneSignal app (e.g. click counts, session duration). Note: data retention is approximately 30 days.

tools.name: view_user
tools.description: Retrieve a user and their properties by alias. Use `alias_label` "external_id" with the user's external ID, or another alias label you have configured.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Deep Research compatible search tool aggregating across sources.

tools.name: fetch

tools.name: search_arxiv
tools.description:
Search academic papers from arXiv.

    Args:
        query: Search query string (e.g., 'machine learning').
        max_results: Maximum number of papers to return (default: 10).
    Returns:
        List of paper metadata in dictionary format.

tools.name: search_pubmed
tools.description:
Search academic papers from PubMed.

    Args:
        query: Search query string (e.g., 'machine learning').
        max_results: Maximum number of papers to return (default: 10).
    Returns:
        List of paper metadata in dictionary format.

tools.name: search_biorxiv
tools.description:
Search academic papers from bioRxiv.

    Args:
        query: Search query string (e.g., 'machine learning').
        max_results: Maximum number of papers to return (default: 10).
    Returns:
        List of paper metadata in dictionary format.

tools.name: search_medrxiv
tools.description:
Search academic papers from medRxiv.

    Args:
        query: Search query string (e.g., 'machine learning').
        max_results: Maximum number of papers to return (default: 10).
    Returns:
        List of paper metadata in dictionary format.

tools.name: search_google_scholar

Search academic papers from Google Scholar.

        List of paper metadata in dictionary format.

Download PDF of an arXiv paper.

tools.name: search_semantic
tools.description:
Search academic papers from Semantic Scholar.

    Args:

tools.name: search_crossref
tools.description:
Search academic papers from CrossRef database.
    
    CrossRef is a scholarly infrastructure organization that provides 
    persistent identifiers (DOIs) for scholarly content and metadata.
    It's one of the largest citation databases covering millions of 
    academic papers, journals, books, and other scholarly content.

    Args:
        query: Search query string (e.g., 'machine learning', 'climate change').
        max_results: Maximum number of papers to return (default: 10, max: 1000

tools.name: get_crossref_paper_by_doi
tools.description:
Get a specific paper from CrossRef by its DOI.

    Args:
        doi: Digital Object Identifier (e.g., '10.1038/nature12373').
    Returns:
        Paper metadata in dictionary format, or empty dict if not found.
        
    Example:
        get_crossref_paper_by_doi("10.1038/nature12373")

tools.name: download_crossref
tools.description:
Attempt to download PDF of a CrossRef paper.

    Args:
        paper_id: CrossRef DOI (e.g., '10.1038/nature12373').
        save_path: Directory to save the PDF (default: './downloads').
    Returns:
        str: Message indicating that direct PDF download is not supported.
        
    Note:
        CrossRef is a citation database and doesn't provide direct PDF downloads.
        Use the DOI to access the paper through the publisher's website.

tools.name: read_crossref_paper
tools.description:
Attempt to read and extract text content from a CrossRef paper.

    Args:
        paper_id: CrossRef DOI (e.g., '10.1038/nature12373').
        save_path: Directory where the PDF is/will be saved (default: './downloads').
    Returns:
        str: Message indicating that direct paper reading is not supported.
        
    Note:
        CrossRef is a citation database and doesn't provide direct paper content.
        Use the DOI to access the paper through the publisher's website.

servers.name: macrostrat-mcp
servers.description: Explore global geologic data to answer questions about bedrock, formations, ages, and stratigraphy. Retrieve units, columns, minerals, timescales, and definitions for any location to build accurate geological context. Generate geology map tiles for quick visualization of areas of interest.

tools.name: find-columns
tools.description: Find geological stratigraphic columns, rock layers, and geological history for any location worldwide. Use for geology, bedrock, formations, age dating, and stratigraphic analysis.

tools.name: find-units
tools.description: Find geological rock units, formations, bedrock geology, and mineral information for any location worldwide. Use for geology questions, rock types, age dating, lithology, and geological analysis.

tools.name: defs
tools.description: Routes giving access to standard fields and dictionaries used in Macrostrat

tools.name: defs-autocomplete
tools.description: Quickly retrieve all definitions matching a query. Limited to 100 results

tools.name: mineral-info
tools.description: Get information about a mineral, use one property

tools.name: timescale
tools.description: Get information about a time period

tools.name: lat-lng-to-tile
tools.description: Convert latitude/longitude coordinates to map tile coordinates (x, y) for a given zoom level. Uses the same web mercator projection as MapKit.

tools.name: map-tiles
tools.description: Get map tile URLs from the Macrostrat tiles server. Use lat-lng-to-tile tool first to get proper x,y coordinates. Defaults to 'carto' scale which automatically adapts detail level to zoom.

Get air quality information for a specified city including PM2.5, PM10,

tools.name: get_air_quality_details
